summary: High-risk plugin patterns found: staged-payload, persistence-autorun. Strongest evidence comes from executable signals. Treat as dangerous until reviewed.

packageRecommendation.reason: At least one high-risk group should be blocked by default for this plugin (staged payload with persistence).

kindSensitiveNotes: Plugins have a larger runtime-trust surface. Packaged code, manifests, hooks, and persistence behavior matter more than README-only language.
This plugin shows packaged runtime or persistence-style signals. Review what can execute automatically, not just what the docs say.
At least one grouped risk narrative reached a block-level recommendation for this artifact type.

findings.title: Secondary payload download-and-execute pattern found
findings.summary: This package appears to download a file or archive, make it runnable or unpack it, and then execute the next stage.
findings.recommendation: Block until the downloaded payload is pinned, reviewed, and separated from automatic execution.

findings.title: Persistence or autorun behavior referenced
findings.summary: This package references startup persistence, cron installation, service enablement, or shell-profile modification that could make it stick around after install.
findings.recommendation: Treat as high risk until each persistence mechanism is justified, narrowly scoped, and removable.

groupedFindings.title: Staged payload with persistence
groupedFindings.summary: scripts/install.sh fetches or stages a payload, then tries to keep it resident.
groupedFindings.exploitabilityReason: The evidence includes executable behavior that can run directly during install or script execution.
groupedFindings.recommendedActionReason: This plugin pattern can execute automatically through manifest or packaged runtime behavior, so blocking by default is safest.

topRisks.title: Staged payload with persistence
